content:
A view from the energy industry:
Roughly 15% of TotalEnergies’ production is offline, as the war with Iran nears the one-month mark, but surging oil prices have more than made up for the lost barrels, chairman and CEO Patrick Pouyanné told CNBC in an exclusive interview....
“The Brent market is ok, but the products market, which is the one which impacts customers … is much higher than Brent,” he told CNBC .... He added, the world has “never experienced” refining margins from products including Asian jet fuel at current levels."
#economy #energy #markets #middleeastwar
llm_reasoning: The tweet discusses production cuts and refining margins affecting product markets, which could lead traders to adjust their buying/selling strategies for Crude Oil (WTI) based on expected supply/demand dynamics.
